steps.text: Determine the optimum sampling system configuration for measuring the target analytes.
Select the equipment for extracting and transporting gas samples.
Select the techniques and/or equipment for the measurement of sample pressures and temperatures in the sample cell.
Heat sample transport lines to maintain sample temperature at least 10 °F (5 °C) above the dew point for all sample constituents.
Select a spectroscopic configuration for the application.
Prepare an analysis algorithm for acquired spectra.
Prior to testing, verify that the sample integration time is sufficient to achieve the required signal-to-noise ratio.
Conduct a leak check following the procedures specified in section 11.1.
Verify detector linearity following the procedures specified in section 11.2.
Decrease the aperture or attenuate the IR beam, and repeat the linearity check.
Flow dry nitrogen through the gas cell and verify that no significant amounts of absorbing species are present.
Collect a background spectrum, using a signal averaging period equal to or longer than that being used for averaging of source sample spectra.
Flow the CTS gas through the cell and verify that the measured concentration is stable to within the uncertainty of the gas standard.
Record the CTS spectrum.
Measure the linewidth of appropriate CTS band(s) to verify instrument resolution.
Conduct a QA spike per the instructions in section 11.4.
Perform a stratification check.
Perform sampling using a continuous flow of source gas.
Sample using the '3-point short line'.
Sample using the stratification check procedure specified in section 11.5.1.1.
Monitor the signal transmittance while sampling.
Obtain a new background spectrum.
After the sampling run, record another CTS spectrum.
Perform post-run QA per section 9.1.2.

preconditions.text: If the transmittance (relative to background) changes by 5% or more in any analytical spectral region
If the detector response is not linear
If the criteria for single point sampling is not met, but the concentration at each traverse point differs from the mean concentration by no more than 10% of the mean
If the concentration at any traverse point differs from the mean by more than 10%

constraints.text: Many HAPs measured by this method are suspected toxic or hazardous and may present serious health risks. Exposure to these compounds from stack gas or from spiking standards should be avoided.
Ensure safety data sheets (SDS) for gas standards are available to all personnel using this method.
When using analyte standards, ensure that gases are properly vented and that the gas handling system is leak free.
The leak rate must be equal to or less than 2.5 mmHg per minute.
The measured FWHH of the water peak must be within 5% of the nominal instrument resolution to proceed with testing.
